description: Create granular super admin permission system with role-based access levels, enforce super admin deletion protection, force super admin tenant to enterprise plan, and define 4 proper plan tiers (Free, Basic, Pro, Enterprise) with feature matrices
steps: 1. Create lib/permissions/super-admin-permissions.ts - Define granular super admin permission categories: tenants.view, tenants.manage, tenants.delete, users.view, users.manage, plans.view, plans.manage, modules.view, modules.manage, settings.view, settings.manage, audit.view, billing.view, billing.manage, monitoring.view, backups.view, backups.manage. Include a SUPER_ADMIN_ROLES map with predefined roles: 'super_admin_full' (all permissions), 'super_admin_readonly' (view-only), 'super_admin_tenant_manager' (tenants + users manage), 'super_admin_billing' (billing + plans manage). Each role has a permissions Record<string, boolean>.

2. Add superAdminRoleSlug field to the users table in drizzle/schema/core.ts - Add a text column 'super_admin_role' that defaults to 'super_admin_full' for backward compat. This column is only meaningful when isSuperAdmin is true.

3. Create lib/permissions/super-admin-gate.ts - Export function requireSuperAdminPerm(ctx: AuthContext, permission: string) that returns NextResponse|null. If ctx.isSuperAdmin is false, return 403. Otherwise check the user's super_admin_role against the SUPER_ADMIN_ROLES map from step 1. If the role grants the permission, return null. Otherwise return 403 with descriptive error.

4. Update app/api/superadmin/users/route.ts DELETE handler - Currently it returns 403 'User deletion is disabled'. Enhance it to explicitly check: if the target user has isSuperAdmin=true, return 403 'Super admin users cannot be deleted'. For regular users, also keep deletion disabled (existing behavior). This makes the protection explicit.

5. Update app/api/superadmin/tenants/route.ts - In the DELETE handler, add a check: look up the tenant's ownerId, and if that owner is a super admin user, return 403 'Cannot delete a tenant owned by a super admin'. Also add logic in the GET and PATCH handlers: if the tenant is the super admin's own tenant (matching the requesting user's membership), force planId to 'enterprise' in the response and reject any attempt to change planId away from 'enterprise'.

6. Create lib/plans/plan-definitions.ts - Define the 4 canonical plan tiers with complete feature matrices: FREE (id:'free', $0/mo, 2 users, 500 contacts, 100 deals, 1GB storage, 500 API/day, features: contacts, deals, tasks, calendar, reports), BASIC (id:'basic', $29/mo/$290/yr, 10 users, 5000 contacts, 1000 deals, 5GB storage, 5000 API/day, features: free + automations, forms, quotes, invoices, orders, products, custom_roles), PRO (id:'pro', $79/mo/$790/yr, 25 users, 25000 contacts, 5000 deals, 25GB storage, 25000 API/day, features: basic + sequences, ai, api_access, contracts, subscriptions, audit_logs, dedicated_support), ENTERPRISE (id:'enterprise', $199/mo/$1990/yr, -1 (unlimited) users/contacts/deals, 100GB storage, -1 API/day, features: pro + sso, custom_domain, advanced_security, white_label, priority_support, compliance, data_export). Export as PLAN_DEFINITIONS array and as a lookup map PLAN_MAP.

7. Create app/api/superadmin/plans/seed/route.ts - A POST endpoint (super admin only) that upserts the 4 canonical plans from PLAN_DEFINITIONS into the plans table. This allows initializing or resetting the plan database to the standard 4-tier structure.

8. Update app/superadmin/billing/page.tsx - Replace the hardcoded FEATURE_OPTIONS with imports from the plan definitions. Add a visual indicator showing which plan is the 'enforced' plan for super admin. Show a 'Seed Default Plans' button that calls the seed endpoint.

9. Create app/superadmin/access-control/page.tsx - A new super admin page for managing granular admin permissions. Show a table of all super admin users with their current role. Allow changing roles via a dropdown (super_admin_full, super_admin_readonly, super_admin_tenant_manager, super_admin_billing). Include a permissions matrix view showing what each role can access.

10. Create app/api/superadmin/access-control/route.ts - GET returns all super admin users with their roles. PATCH allows updating a super admin user's role (only the 'super_admin_full' role holder can change other admins' roles).
11. Update app/superadmin/layout.tsx - Add the 'Access Control' link to the super admin navigation. Import the nav items and add the new page.

12. Update FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md - Add a new section documenting: the 4-plan structure with feature matrix, the granular super admin permission system with role definitions, the super admin deletion protection, and the enterprise plan enforcement for super admin tenants.
acceptance_criteria: lib/permissions/super-admin-permissions.ts exports SUPER_ADMIN_PERMISSIONS array and SUPER_ADMIN_ROLES map with 4 predefined roles
lib/permissions/super-admin-gate.ts exports requireSuperAdminPerm function that enforces granular permissions
lib/plans/plan-definitions.ts exports PLAN_DEFINITIONS with 4 plans (free, basic, pro, enterprise) each with limits and features
app/api/superadmin/users/route.ts DELETE explicitly rejects deletion of super admin users with specific error message
app/api/superadmin/tenants/route.ts DELETE rejects deletion of tenants owned by super admin users
app/api/superadmin/plans/seed/route.ts can upsert the 4 canonical plans into the database
app/superadmin/access-control/page.tsx renders the granular admin permissions UI
app/api/superadmin/access-control/route.ts handles GET and PATCH for admin role management
FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md is updated with new sections for plans and permissions
verification: Run npx tsc --noEmit from the project root and confirm no type errors in new/modified files
Run npx vitest run to confirm existing tests still pass
Grep for the new exports to confirm they exist: SUPER_ADMIN_PERMISSIONS, SUPER_ADMIN_ROLES, PLAN_DEFINITIONS, requireSuperAdminPerm
